name: Low-Calorie Potato, Onion, and Garlic Gratin
recipeIngredient: 4 large Baking potatoes, baked, scooped out and mashed
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the papery covering of the garlic heads without peeling or separating cloves. Wrap in foil and place in oven.
2. Place onions on double layer of foil; do not wrap. Bake with garlic at 425°F for 1 hour.
3. After 1 hour, remove garlic heads, let cool 5 minutes, then separate cloves and press into a bowl to release softened garlic.
4. After 1¼ hours, check onions. If not very soft (almost collapsed), return to oven briefly.
5. Trim stem and root ends of onions; peel off skin and first layer. Pulse in food processor until smooth.
6. Lower temperature to 350°F. Combine puréed onions, garlic, potatoes, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
7. Stir in buttermilk. Transfer mixture to a gratin dish, smoothing top. Sprinkle with cheese.
8. Refrigerate prepared dish up to 2 days; bring to room temp before proceeding.
9. Drizzle milk over potatoes. Bake uncovered at 350°F for 35-45 minutes or until brown, bubbly, and hot.
10. Serve immediately.
